PHYS332 Physics for Life Sciences II (4 Credits)

The second part of a two-semester course in general physics specifically oriented towards applications relevant for students in biology and pre- medical programs. The course covers basic statistical physics, electricity and magnetism, and optics done in authentic biological contexts. Prerequisite: PHYS331; or PHYS131; or students who have taken courses with comparable content may contact the department.

Credit Only Granted for. PHYS122, PHYS132, or PHYS332.
